title: Wan workflows: stage-2 sigma drift — apply the LTX low-start-sigma refine fix
description: The Wan i2v/t2v two-stage workflows show BAD stage-1 -> stage-2 inconsistency (very different results, especially t2v). Same root cause we just fixed on LTX-2.3 (MPI-4): stage-2 starts denoising too HIGH, so it REGENERATES instead of REFINING. On Wan the stage-2 path uses BasicScheduler (steps 6, denoise 1.00) + SplitSigmas (step 3) -> high-denoise stage-2 -> drift. The LTX fix: feed stage-2 a LOW start sigma (LTX landed on manual sigmas 0.65,0.45,0.25,0.0 = the 'knee' where stage-2 refines without re-deciding content; 0.5 too soft, 0.7+ drifts audio/identity, 0.85 over-sharp+plasticky). Apply the analogous low-start-denoise refine to Wan stage-2 (likely lower the BasicScheduler denoise from 1.0, or switch stage-2 to manual sigmas with a low start), and sweep on HUMAN faces (animated content hides the difference). Verify stage-1 vs stage-2 are CONSISTENT (refine, not regenerate). Note: tune per Wan's sampler/scheduler (curve shape matters at few steps). See brief.md + docs/builder/research/audio-input.md (LTX sigma sweep) for the method.
